head_templates.name: head
head_templates.expansion: იყო და არა იყო რა • (iq̇o da ara iq̇o ra)

etymology_text:
Literally, “there was and there was not”.
Compare the similar semantics of Azerbaijani biri var idi, biri yox idi (“once upon a time/there was, there was not”), Turkish bir varmış, bir yokmuş (“once upon a time/there was, there was not”), Persian یکی بود یکی نبود (yeki bud yeki nabud, “once upon a time/there was, there was not”), Armenian լինում է, չի լինում (linum ē, čʻi linum) and կար-չկար (kar-čʻkar, “once upon a time/there was, there was not”).

args.1: there was and there was not

head_templates.name: head
head_templates.expansion: იყო და არა იყო რა, ღვთის უკეთესი რა იქნებოდა • (iq̇o da ara iq̇o ra, ɣvtis uḳetesi ra ikneboda)

etymology_text: A larger variant of იყო და არა იყო რა (iq̇o da ara iq̇o ra). It literally means "there was and there was not, there was not anything better than Lord". Compare Persian یکی بود، یکی نبود، غیر از خدا هیچ کس نبود

etymology_text: Literally, “to sweep/wipe from the face of the earth.” A calque of the Ancient Greek ἐξαλείψω... ἀπὸ προσώπου τῆς γῆς (exaleípsō... apò prosṓpou tês gês) found in the Old Georgian translation of the Bible, specifically Genesis 6:7 and 7:4.

etymology_templates.name: der
etymology_templates.expansion: Ancient Greek ἐξαλείψω... ἀπὸ προσώπου τῆς γῆς (exaleípsō... apò prosṓpou tês gês)

etymology_text: From წინააღმდეგ (c̣inaaɣmdeg, “opposite, against”) + შემთხვევაში (šemtxvevaši, “in the case”), which is the locative-like postpositional inflected singular form of შემთხვევა (šemtxveva).

categories.name: Georgian entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From ამ (am, “this”) + შემთხვევაში (šemtxvevaši, “in the case”), which is the locative singular of შემთხვევა (šemtxveva).

glosses: in this case, in this instance, in this event

categories.name: Georgian entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From სხვა (sxva, “other”) + შემთხვევაში (šemtxvevaši, “in the case”), which is the locative singular of შემთხვევა (šemtxveva).
